topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 87.2 Starter grade with Nico Collins comp; likely on the board at 1.09 after Tate/Lemon/Love come off. Geeks are WR-heavy (9) but their affinity is medium so they'll still take a top-10 RSP board talent over a need pick if BPA is this clear.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: RSP RB3 (DoT 84.6) addresses the thin RB room (only 4 rostered). Sliding ADP makes him plausible at 1.09 or as a trade-back target; cap-friendly rookie deal fits the $5.9M room.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Starter grade (DoT 86.3) and ADP 13.57 puts him in range at 1.09. Medium RSP affinity means they trust the board; consensus and RSP align here, lowering risk.

topTargets.name: Demond Claiborne
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 84.5 with Shady McCoy comp — fits as a 2.09 lottery ticket on a thin RB room. ADP slips him to round 2 due to weight concerns, exactly the value Geeks chase with medium RSP lean.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: RSP TE2 (DoT 84.4) with ADP 21.25 lines up as a 3.09 target. Geeks only have 2 TEs and 5 contracts expiring — adding a cheap rookie TE on a multi-year deal hedges roster turnover.

capPosture: Tight but workable at $5.9M space; can active-roster all three rookies if they stay in expected slots, with one possible taxi stash to preserve flexibility against the 5 expiring contracts.

wildcard.name: Fernando Mendoza
wildcard.reasoning: RSP QB1 (DoT 85.6) at 1.09 would be a reach, but Geeks roster only 2 QBs with contracts churning. Medium RSP affinity could tip them toward Waldman's top overall prospect if WR/RB runs clear the board.

summary: Gridiron Geeks enter 1.09 with a glaring positional imbalance — 9 WRs but only 4 RBs and 2 TEs — and $5.9M of cap room to absorb rookie deals. Expect them to take the best available RB/TE/WR per their blended RSP+consensus board, with Fields, Johnson, or Concepcion as the realistic 1.09. Rounds 2-3 will target a thunder back (Claiborne) and a developmental TE (Stowers) to stabilize the depth chart against 5 expiring contracts.
